title: Intricately Garlicky Turkish Tempeh Borek
description: A garlicky, aerated Turkish dish featuring tempeh, perfect for a date night, this shojin-friendly borek is a unique and delicious experience. It combines the flavors of garlic, tempeh, and spices in a flaky pastry.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the crumbled tempeh and cook for 5 minutes, until lightly browned.
Stir in the paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Unroll the phyllo dough and cut it into 4 equal pieces.
Place a quarter of the tempeh mixture onto the center of each piece of phyllo dough.
Brush the edges with lemon juice and fold the dough into triangles.
Place the borek on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush with olive oil.
Bake for 25 minutes, until golden brown.
